title: panicum rigidulum var. pubescens
intro: Panicum rigidulum var. pubescens, also known as stiff panic grass, is a type of grass native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, which have been traditionally used to treat wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of stiff panic grass include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and promote wound healing, making it a valuable resource for herbal remedies. Stiff panic grass can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and can also be dried and used in poultices or compresses to treat various health conditions.
